domain: Maasai ilpayiani elder council (age-graded leadership; consensus-not-majority decision; discussion continues until basin-stable agreement; laibon spiritual authority weight)
specific_mechanism: Age-weighted ballots with repeated-round discussion until basin-stable consensus emerges; weighted voices converge on a position over multiple rounds, terminated by stability not majority count.
llm_application: ILPAYIANI-CONSENSUS: an age-weighted basin-stable multi-agent consensus protocol. Each agent i has prior-calibrated confidence weight w_i derived from past task performance on similar tasks. At round t, agents vote with weighted ballots producing distribution P_t. Stability detector compares P_t with P_{t-1}, ..., P_{t-K} using Kolmogorov-Smirnov distance; protocol terminates when KS-distance below ε for K consecutive rounds (basin-stable). Differs from Ranked Voting (single round), Multi-Agent Debate (Beta-Binomial stability) by combining (a) age-calibration weights AND (b) basin-stability termination using KS-distance over rolling window.
novelty_claim: Ranked Voting Self-Consistency uses IRV/Borda/MRR voting (single-round). Multi-Agent Debate with Adaptive Stability Detection (Vusd1Hw2D9) uses Beta-Binomial mixture + KS-test for stability. RECONCILE uses confidence-weighted voting. Debate-or-Vote compares debate vs vote. ILPAYIANI-CONSENSUS recombines these into (a) PRIOR-CALIBRATED age-weighted vote + (b) KS-basin-stability termination + (c) MULTI-ROUND refinement. Recombination of existing components; not a wholly new artefact.
